item.title: Mauritius weather bulletin – 24 March 2026
item.description: Weather news for Mauritius issued at 11h45 on Tuesday 24 March 2026.General situation:A light airstream is flowing over the region.Forecast for the next 24 hours:Cloud developments are expected this afternoon with localised showers to the West, South-West and over the Central Plateau. Partly cloudy elsewhere.These showers may be temporarily moderate.The maximum temperature will be slightly above the mean and will be between 26 and 29 degrees Celsius over the highgrounds and between 31 and 34 degrees Celsius over the coastal regions.Partly cloudy tonight and tomorrow morning becoming cloudy at times to the East and over the highgrounds with passing showers.The minimum temperature will be between 21 and 23 degrees Celsius over the central plateau and between 25 and 27 degrees Celsius along the coasts.Wind will blow from Eastern sector at about 15 km/h.Sea moderate beyond the reefs with Southwesterly swells of the order of 2 metres.High tides :17h47 and tomorrow 04h04.Low tides :23h20 and tomorrow 12h28.Sunset :18h18.Sunrise (tomorrow) :06h15.

item.title: Unclear if Trump is telling truth about talks with Iran or if it's 'bluff'
item.description:
[France 24] Unclear if Trump is telling truth about talks with Iran or if it's 'bluff' US President Donald Trump has said that the US and Iran are having 'very good' talks regarding the war. But they were denied by Tehran, although they did say they had received a request from the White House. There is also the question of where talks would be held - with Pakistan coming out as the most likely location. But the major question is: 'is Trump telling the truth about points of agreement, or is it bluff?' asks FRANCE 24's Kethevane Gorjestani. URL: https://www.france24.com/en/unclear-if-trump-is-telling-truth-about-talks-with-iran-or-if-it-s-bluff
[Al Jazeera] Iran says no talks with US, calls Trump statements a ‘bluff’ Iranian officials have dismissed statements by Trump as a ‘bluff’, denying any direct or indirect talks with the US. URL: https://www.aljazeera.com/video/newsfeed/2026/3/24/iran-says-no-talks-with-us-calls-trump-statements-a?traffic_source=rss
[Africanews] Trump claims ‘very strong’ Iran talks as Tehran denies contact President Donald Trump said Monday the U.S. was talking with a “top" and "respected” Iranian leader and claimed the Islamic Republic was eager for a deal to end the war. URL: http://www.africanews.com/2026/03/23/trump-claims-very-strong-iran-talks-as-tehran-denies-contact/

item.title: For Israel, war against Iran is not over as Trump's attempt to reach deal
item.description:
[France 24] For Israel, war against Iran is not over as Trump's attempt to reach deal Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said Israel would be ready to protect its vital interests under any circumstances, while it continues to strike Iran and Lebanon. This comes as US President Donald Trump has claimed talks were underway with Iran - despite Tehran denying those talks. 'The question is if the US pull out of the war, can Israel carry on with it', says FRANCE 24's Kethevane Gorjestani. item.title: Australia and EU agree sweeping trade deal in face of global uncertainty
item.description:
[BBC News] Australia and EU agree sweeping trade deal in face of global uncertainty Australia and the EU sign sweeping trade and security deals after years of negotiations. URL: https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/cly6g6l6lq7o?at_medium=RSS&at_campaign=rss
[France 24] EU signs trade deal with Australia as it looks for new partners amid tension with US The European Union and Australia struck a long-awaited free-trade deal on March 24, while also agreeing to boost defence cooperation and access to crucial rare-earth minerals in the face of global uncertainty over trade. It's the latest Brussels has agreed to, to diversify trade. But the deal was slammed by EU farmers, who have called 'unacceptable'. FRANCE 24's Dave Keating has more from Brussels. URL: https://www.france24.com/en/video/20260324-eu-signs-trade-deal-with-australia-as-it-looks-for-new-partners-amid-tension-with-us-1
[France 24] EU, Australia seal free trade deal eight years in the making The EU and Australia have signed a sweeping free trade agreement that will eliminate tariffs on nearly all trade between them as global economies seek to hedge against chaotic US tariff policies. Also in this edition: crude oil prices rise again as traders remain sceptical of signals that Washington is trying to de-escalate its war with Iran. URL: https://www.france24.com/en/tv-shows/business/20260324-eu-australia-seal-free-trade-deal-eight-years-in-the-making
[France 24] EU and Australia sign sweeping free trade pact and agree to boost defence ties EU and Australia sign sweeping free trade pact and agree to boost defence ties URL: https://www.france24.com/en/europe/20260324-eu-australia-landmark-free-trade-deal-global-tensions
item.lead_title: Australia and EU agree sweeping trade deal in face of global uncertainty

item.title: «Parlement citoyen – Readers Question Time» : La parole aux lecteurs
item.description: Avec sa nouvelle rubrique «Parlement citoyen – Readers Question Time», l’express renforce son engagement en faveur du débat public. L’objectif est clair : offrir aux citoyens une plateforme pour interpeller directement les élus sur des sujets d’intérêt national. Chaque mardi, des questions envoyées par les lecteurs sont sélectionnées par la rédaction et adressées aux responsables concernés. Ces derniers sont invités à répondre publiquement dans les colonnes du journal. ...

item.title: France : Une Mauricienne à l’honneur aux élections municipales en France
item.description: Dans la commune de Conlie, près du Mans, notre compatriote Mylène Sookahet-Lebegue vient d’être brillamment élue au conseil municipal, dès le premier tour. Bien qu’ayant vécu longtemps en France, ce n’est que tout récemment qu’elle s’est installée dans cette commune. Au-delà d’un simple fait divers, c’est un fait de société qui mérite d’être signalé quand on connaît la difficulté des étrangers ici à se faire reconnaître comme un atout, non comme un problème, s ...

item.description:
[L'Express (via mega.mu)] Affaire Pavaday : Détention prolongée en attendant les plaignantes à l’étranger Un mois après son arrestation, l’affaire Frédéric Pavaday est toujours examinée au tribunal de Curepipe. item.title: Bureau Politique du MMM : Reza Uteem évoque une réaction «humaine» après la démission de Paul Bérenger
item.description: Le ton adopté vendredi dernier par plusieurs dirigeants du MMM à la suite de la démission de Paul Bérenger comme *Deputy Prime Minister* (DPM) était une réaction «humaine» et «émotionnelle». C’est ce qu’a soutenu Reza Uteem, président du parti, lors du point de presse tenu hier, à l’issue de la réunion du BP. Interrogé sur la sévérité du ton adopté à l’égard du leader mauve après ses décennies de militantisme, Reza Uteem a d’abord tenté de contourner la ques ...

item.title: Tensions politiques : La crise au sein du MMM et ce qu’elle signifie pour le cœur et l’âme de la démocratie à Maurice
item.description: Le débat public à Maurice ces derniers temps m’a profondément attristé pour mon pays. Il est devenu plus qu’évident pour moi, au cours des derniers jours, que les Mauriciens, de manière générale, ne comprennent pas ce que sont les principes de la démocratie, ce qu’ils signifient réellement et comment ils s’appliquent. Pour moi, cela signifie malheureusement qu’il n’y aura probablement pas de véritable changement à Maurice avant plusieurs générations — du moins t ...

item.title: Au cœur de la crise mauve
item.description: Le MMM traverse aujourd’hui une crise de celles qui ne surgissent pas par accident. Elles s’installent, sédimentent, se taisent… avant de revenir brûler ce qu’elles n’avaient fait qu’entamer. Ce n’est pas une simple querelle d’ego entre 16 élus dressés contre les deux Bérenger. C’est une répétition historique. Une combustion lente dont l’origine remonte, au moins, à 1993. En août 1993, Paul Bérenger est évincé du gouvernement dirigé par Anerood Jugnauth, lui-m ...

item.title: Tensions à la rue Ambrose : «Vander!» hurle la foule hostile au Bureau politique du MMM
item.description: À Rose-Hill, à la rue Ambrose, hier en fin d’aprèsmidi, l’atmosphère était lourde bien avant le début du Bureau politique (BP) prévu à 17 heures. Dès 16h30, ministres, députés et militants étaient déjà sur place. Des petits groupes discutent à voix basse. On observe, on attend. Puis, les premiers éclats de voix se font entendre. item.title: Emtel dépasse les Rs 4 milliards de revenus
item.description: L’opérateur Emtel a franchi un cap important en enregistrant plus de Rs 4 milliards de revenus pour l’exercice financier clos au 31 décembre 2025, confirmant une dynamique de croissance soutenue. Cette performance, en hausse par rapport à l’année précédente, s’explique notamment par une augmentation de la base de clients et une adoption accrue des services mobiles, résidentiels, d’entreprise et fintech. item.title: Des policiers de l’immigration envoyés en renfort dans quatorze aéroports américains
item.description: Face à la paralysie budgétaire partielle, le gouvernement américain a mis sa menace à exécution. Des agents de la police américaine de l’immigration (ICE) ont été déployés, lundi 23 mars, dans 14 aéroports du pays pour répondre à une pénurie de personnel de sécurité, selon Tom Homan, conseiller de Donald Trump sur l’immigration cité par la chaîne CNN. « Et il y en aura plus », a-t-il ajouté. item.title: La volte-face de Donald Trump qui évoque désormais des négociations avec l’Iran
item.description: Il y a encore 48 heures, Donald Trump ne voulait pas entendre parler d’un cessez-le-feu. Pourtant, à la surprise générale, ce matin, le président américain annonce des négociations avec l’Iran. « Nous avons des discussions solides avec eux et on va voir où ça nous mène. On va leur laisser cinq jours et si ça se passe bien, on scellera un accord. Sinon, on continuera à bombarder à tout va », a déclaré le président américain. item.title: Denmark votes in close election overshadowed by Trump's Greenland bid
item.description:
[France 24] Denmark votes in close election overshadowed by Trump's Greenland bid Denmark votes in early general elections on Tuesday with centre-left Prime Minister Mette Frederiksen hoping her staunch line against US President Donald Trump's push to annex Greenland will carry her to a third term in office. Overseas seats held by Denmark's autonomous territories, including Greenland, could tip the balance if the election result is close. URL: https://www.france24.com/en/europe/20260324-denmark-votes-in-close-election-overshadowed-by-trump-s-greenland-bid
[France 24] Denmark goes to the polls following crisis with US over Greenland Denmark will hold the parliamentary election ​on March 24, with opinion polls showing a narrow lead for Prime Minister Mette Frederiksen's centre-left bloc, who has led Denmark since 2019. US President Donald Trump's push to takeover the semi autonomous Danish territory is likely to play a role in voter's minds along with cost of living issues. URL: https://www.france24.com/en/denmark-goes-to-the-polls-following-crisis-with-us-over-greenland
[Al Jazeera] Polls open in key Denmark elections clouded by Trump’s Greenland threat Prime Minister Mette Frederiksen is projected to win a third term after standing up to the US president over Greenland. URL: https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2026/3/24/polls-open-in-key-denmark-elections-clouded-by-trumps-greenland-threat?traffic_source=rss

item.title: Colombian military plane crash kills at least 66
item.description:
[BBC News] Colombian military plane crash kills at least 66 Colombia's defence minister says it was a "tragic accident" but the cause is not yet clear. URL: https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/c8dlmrjn1l2o?at_medium=RSS&at_campaign=rss
[France 24] Colombian military plane crashes, killing at least 66 A military transport plane crashed in Colombia on March 24, killing at least 66 passengers. The Lockheed Martin Hercules C-130 plane that reportedly suffered an impact near the runway as it took off from a a city near the Peruvian border, fell from the sky in a remote area, impeding rescue efforts. The event prompted calls from Colombian President Gustavo Petro to modernise the country's ageing military--a process he says has been hamstrung by bureaucratic obstacles. URL: https://www.france24.com/en/colombian-military-plane-crashes-killing-at-least-66
[France 24] Colombian military transport plane crashes with 121 on board, killing scores A military transport plane carrying 121 people, mostly soldiers, crashed shortly after takeoff on Monday in Puerto Leguizamo, Colombia, killing at least 66 and injuring 81, authorities said. URL: https://www.france24.com/en/americas/20260324-military-plane-crash-colombia-121-onboard-dozens-dead

item.title: North Korea’s Kim Jong-un says nuclear-armed status ‘irreversible’
item.description: North Korea will never relinquish its status as a nuclear-armed state, state media reported leader Kim Jong-un as saying on Tuesday. Speaking after his reappointment to head the State Affairs Commission, Kim called the policy "irreversible" and vowed to intensify efforts against what he described as "hostile forces".

item.title: EU 'greatly concerned' by reports Hungary leaked negotiation details to Russia
item.description: The European Commission said Monday it was “greatly concerned” by reports Hungary’s foreign minister shared sensitive EU negotiation details with Russia. The Washington Post cited officials saying Hungarian Foreign Minister Peter Szijjarto briefed Russian Foreign Minister Sergei Lavrov during meetings. Szijjarto dismissed the report as “fake news” and “senseless conspiracy theories”.

item.title: Misleading campaign adverts target French far left in local elections
item.description: Over the weekend, thousands of people in Toulouse – France's fourth-largest city – were exposed to a misleading campaign targeting Francois Piquemal, the far-left France Unbowed party's candidate for mayor, ahead of Sunday's municipal elections. It happened in the middle of France’s electoral silence period, raising suspicions of foreign interference. Emerald Maxwell explains in this edition of Truth or Fake.

item.title: Oil plunges, stocks surge on Trump's Iran reprieve

[Defimedia] Bureau politique du MMM : des militants pro-Bérenger font entendre leur voix dans la rue C’était un Bureau politique particulier qui s’est tenu au siège du MMM ce lundi. D’une part l’absence de Paul Bérenger, URL: https://defimedia.info/bureau-politique-du-mmm-des-militants-pro-berenger-font-entendre-leur-voix-dans-la-rue

item.title: CcHUB launches GATEWAY to train 340,000 Nigerians for global gig work
item.description: CcHUB wants to train 340,000 Nigerians across 10 states in digital marketing, graphic design, UI/UX design, and video production and editing.

item.title: Missing Man Found Safe After 2-Day Search in Plaine-Champagne
item.description: A 73-year-old man has been rescued by a police helicopter this Monday after spending two nights missing in the dense terrain of Plaine-Champagne. Hervé Valère, a resident of New-Grove, was located in the early afternoon on Monday, 23 March. The … The post Missing Man Found Safe After 2-Day Search in Plaine-Champagne appeared first on News Moris.

item.title: Guinea cracks down on suspected GSIM networks
item.description: Guinean authorities have dismantled a suspected terrorist network linked to the Group for the Support of Islam and Muslims (GSIM), arresting multiple foreign and Guinean nationals as part of a nationwide anti-terror operation.

item.title: UN forces withdraw from two Central African bases, passing infrastructure to state authorities
item.description: The United Nations has announced the closure of two temporary operating bases in the Central African Republic (CAR), marking a significant adjustment in its peacekeeping footprint

item.title: 'Do not doubt we will do this': Iran warns US against carrying out threat to strike power plants
item.description: Iran on Monday said it would strike electrical plants across the Middle East if the US follows through on a threat to bomb power stations in the Islamic Republic.

item.title: China set to cut solar incentives for Africa days after Elon Musk negotiates $2.9B Chinese solar equipment deal for Tesla
item.description: Africa’s solar expansion could face new cost pressures as China moves to scale back key export incentives, just as global demand surges following Elon Musk’s $2

item.title: World experiences hottest 11 years on record, WMO warns
item.description: The world has just recorded its hottest 11-year period, according to a new report from the World Meteorological Organization.

item.title: Niger civil groups condemn EU call to free ex-president Bazoum
item.description: Civil society organisations in Niger have condemned a European Union resolution demanding the release of the country’s former president. They're calling for a simultaneous mobilisation in Niger, Burkina Faso and Mali to protest the move.

item.title: France's former Socialist Prime Minister Lionel Jospin dies at 88
item.description: Jospin was head of government from 1997 to 2002 and is credited with bringing in the 35-hour working week.
